subject: Re: running WARP in Linux
body:
Hi Yan,
Warp desktop will unfortunately not run on Linux, it was built with a UI framework called windows presentation foundation and is windows specific.
Instead, you now have access to the algorithms in Warp through a command line tool called WarpTools - here is the documentation: 
https://warpem.com/user_guide/warptools/quick_start_warptools_tilt_series/
If you run into any issues please open an issue 
https://github.com/warpem/warp
Cheers and happy processing,
Alister
Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
On Oct 11, 2024, at 02:39, yah...@gmail.com <yahuiuk@gmail.com> wrote:
﻿Hello,
I used to us the WARP 1.0 on Windows and now am switching to WARP 2.0 on Ubuntu. I have followed the Github pape and installed WARP in a conda environment. How shall I launch the graphic WARP viewer? Or does it run by command lines and where to find a sheet of command lines?  Sorry for the silly questions.
Thanks a lot!
Kind regards,
Yan
